message:
fix(db,server): ensure recipe schema before every operation and refresh TUI metadata DB connection on URL change

Recipe operations could fail with "no such table" errors if the metadata
DB was created without recipe tables (e.g. by an older version or a
different codepath). Additionally, the TUI search screen cached a single
metadata DB connection but never invalidated it when the database URL
changed, causing stale reads and writes to the wrong database.

- Add ensure_recipe_schema() that runs all recipe migrations before
  every public function in search_recipes.rs: insert, update, delete,
  list, get, touch, prune, count for both recipes and history (12 call
  sites total), ensuring schema existence regardless of DB origin
- Add metadata_database_url field to TUI search screen state to track
  which DB URL the cached connection belongs to
- Invalidate cached metadata_db_conn and reset recipes_loaded flag when
  ensure_metadata_db_conn detects a database URL change
- Add open_live_metadata_operation_db_connection() for write operations,
  opening a fresh connection instead of reusing the cached read connection
- Route all recipe/history write paths (save, load, touch) through
  fresh connections, then store back in metadata_db_conn for subsequent
  reads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
